Personnel and Training) NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 29th December, 2015 G.S.R. 1012(E).—In exercise of the powers conferred by sub-section (1) of Section 3 of the All India Services Act, 1951 (61 of 1951), read with sub-rule (2) of rule 4 of the Indian Administrative Service (Cadre) Rules, 1954, the Central Government, in consultation with the Government of Jharkhand, hereby makes the following regulations further to amend the Indian Administrative Service (Fixation of Cadre Strength) Regulations, 1955, namely:— -- 3 of 9 -- 4 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(i)] 1. (i) These Regulations may be called the Indian Administrative Service (Fixation of Cadre Strength) Third Amendment Regulations, 2015. (ii) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. In the schedule to the Indian Administrative Service (Fixation of Cadre Strength) Regulations, 1955, for the heading “Jharkhand” and the(E)ntries occurring there under, the following shall be substituted namely:— “JHARKHAND Senior Duty Posts under the State Government 117 Chief Secretary to Government 1 Development Commissioner 1 Member, Board of Revenue 1 Director General, Sri Krishna Institute of Public Administration 1 Principal Secretary 06 Principal Secretary to Governor 01 Principal Secretary to Chief Minister 01 Chief(E)lectoral Officer 01 Agriculture Production Commissioner 01 Divisional Commissioner 05 Secretary to Government 22 Spl./Addl./Jt./Dy.Secretary 22 Tribal Welfare Commissioner 01 Director Industries 01 I.G. Posts to be filled up by Direct Recruitment (Items 1+2+3+4+5-6) 150 Total Authorized Strength 215” [F. No. 11031/07/2015- AIS-II (A)] RAJIV JAIN, Under Secy. Note 1: Prior to the issue of this notification, the Total Authorized Strength of Jharkhand IAS Cadre was 208 . Note 2: The principal Regulations were published in the Gazette of India vide SRO No. 3350, dated 22.10.1955. (i) These rules may be called the Indian Administrative Service (Pay) Third Amendment Rules, 2015. (ii) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the official Gazette. 2. In the Indian Administrative Service (Pay) Rules, 2007:- In “Schedule II-Part A” posts carrying pay above the senior scale of pay of the Indian Administrative Service under the State Governments, in the table, for the(E)ntry “Jharkhand” occurring in the first column and corresponding(E)ntries in the second column, the following shall be substituted namely:— “Jharkhand Chief Secretary to Government Rs. 80000/-(fixed) Development Commissioner Rs. 80000/-(fixed) Member, Board of Revenue Rs. 80000/-(fixed) Director General, Sri Krishna Institute of Public Administration Rs. 80000/-(fixed) Principal Secretary HAG Scale – Rs. 67000/-(annual increment @ 3%)- 79000/- Principal Secretary to Governor HAG Scale – Rs. 67000/-(annual increment @ 3%)- 79000/- Principal Secretary to Chief Minister HAG Scale – Rs. 67000/-(annual increment @ 3%)- 79000/- Chief(E)lectoral Officer HAG Scale – Rs. 67000/-(annual increment @ 3%)- 79000/- Agriculture Production Commissioner HAG Scale – Rs. 67000/-(annual increment @ 3%)- 79000/- . Divisional Commissioner PB-4 + GP Rs.10000/- Secretary to Government PB-4 + GP Rs.10000/-” (b) In “Schedule II - Part B” Posts carrying pay in the senior scale of the Indian Administrative Service under the State Governments including posts carrying special allowance in addition to pay, for the(E)ntries occurring under ‘Jharkhand’, the following shall be substituted, namely:- “Spl./Addl./Jt./Dy.Secretary Tribal Welfare Commissioner Director Industries I.G. Prison I.G.
